No Poverty.reason: Course discusses tax reforms which can influence poverty reduction policies.

Quality Education.reason: Course aims to enhance students' analytical skills in taxation, linking to education quality.
evidence: 發展以財政、統計及經濟基本理論為基礎之分析能力

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course covers taxation's impact on economic issues, relevant to decent work.
evidence: 解析與所得稅有密切關連之經濟與產業議題

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Taxation discussions may address economic disparities.

No Poverty.reason: The study of income tax and fiscal policy is foundational to funding social protection systems (Target 1.3) and creating pro-poor policy frameworks (Target 1.b).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course on fiscal policy and tax systems relates to how governments fund public services, including health financing (Target 3.c) and universal health coverage (Target 3.8).

Quality Education.reason: Understanding fiscal policy and taxation is relevant to the public funding mechanisms that support education systems, an inferred link to achieving quality education for all.

Gender Equality.reason: Tax "policy debates" could include discussions on gender-sensitive tax reforms (Target 5.c), but this is a weak inference as it is not explicitly mentioned.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly analyzes how income tax policy impacts "economic and industrial issues" and corporate finance, aligning with development-oriented policies for economic growth (Targets 8.1, 8.3).
evidence: 解析與所得稅有密切關連之經濟與產業議題

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course analyzes "economic and industrial issues" related to income tax, directly connecting to policies that influence industrialization and innovation (Targets 9.2, 9.b).
evidence: 解析與所得稅有密切關連之經濟與產業議題

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Progressive income tax is a primary fiscal policy for equality (Target 10.4). The course's focus on "Personal Income Taxation" and "policy debates" directly addresses this.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course on tax systems is fundamental to building effective and transparent institutions (Target 16.6). "International Trends" in taxation often address illicit financial flows (Target 16.4).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is fundamentally about income taxation, which is the core of "domestic resource mobilization" and improving "domestic capacity for tax... collection" as stated in Target 17.1.
